body.title: CLOSED: ADC MCP tool: shell_exec — run shell commands from any device
body.result: shell_exec() MCP tool, machine-level protection, 5 tests
body.description:
No MCP tool exists for any ADC device to run shell commands. This blocks workspace manipulation, running tests, grepping code, checking logs, and all autonomous workflow execution.

Proposed shape: Add `shell_exec(cmd, cwd=None, timeout_s=30)` to Librarian tools. Uses subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=cwd, timeout=timeout_s). Returns {stdout, stderr, exit_code, cmd, cwd, timed_out}. Logs to action_log (exit_code included). Protection model: machine-level — this machine is dedicated to the Igor project, no path whitelist needed. Cap timeout at 300s max.

**Affected files:** new `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/exec_tools.py`, update `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/__init__.py`
**Design rules:** no-TheIgors-imports; action-log-every-call; shell=True documented as intentional (machine-level protection, dedicated machine)
**Scope boundary:** shell_exec only. Synchronous, no streaming, no background process management.
**Test plan:** shell_exec('echo hello') → {stdout:'hello\n', exit_code:0}; shell_exec('false') → {exit_code:1}; shell_exec('sleep 999', timeout_s=0.1) → {timed_out:True}; action_log entry present.
